Physical AI Engineering NotesRobotics, ROS 2, and Long-Term Systems

Robotics Control, ROS 2, Humanoid Systems, and Long-Term Engineering Notes

Tech Logs
Articles
Projects

Robotics Control

Whole-body coordination, model-based and learning-backed controllers, and safe interaction with unstructured environments.

ROS 2 Systems

Nodes, executors, lifecycle, DDS concerns, and integration patterns for fieldable robot software.

Humanoid Manipulation

Contact-rich tasks, dexterous planning, and closing the loop from perception to reliable execution.

Physical AI Data Pipelines

Logging, labeling, simulation-to-real workflows, and datasets that respect real-world constraints.

Long-Term Engineering Career

Depth over hype, reproducible notes, and systems that remain understandable years later.

Welcome ​

I am Harrison Woo, a robotics software engineer. This site collects working notes, public write-ups, and project summaries at the intersection of robot control, ROS 2, manipulation, and Physical AI systems.

I use it as a long-term engineering archive: a place where weekly logs, deeper articles, and career reflections can accumulate without losing structure.

Explore ​

  • Tech Logs — concise weekly records of problems, fixes, and lessons.
  • Articles — longer technical pieces organized by topic.
  • Projects — portfolio-style summaries of significant work.

For background and how I think about the craft, see About. For books, papers, and references, see Reading and Career notes.

Archive statement ​

These pages are written for clarity over time. They favor honest tradeoffs, reproducible reasoning, and material that will still be useful after the next hardware generation ships.

Built as a long-term engineering archive.
